properties.remark: OBSERVER REPORTS TWO PINE TREES AND POWER POLES DOWN. POWER OUTAGE AT RESIDENCE.
SEVERE THUNDERSTORMS. REPORTS OUT OF AMERICAN FALLS INDICATE ROOF OF BUILDING BLOWN OFF INTO POWER LINES. POWER OUT IN PARTS OF AMERICAN FALLS.
SUSTAINED WINDS AT 50 MPH
61 MPH WIND GUST
FLOODING WAS REPORTED ALONG BADGER CREEK AND IS FLOWING OVER LOCAL ROADS. FLOWING WATER WAS ESTIMATED TO BE ABOUT 16 INCHES DEEP.
ROCKS AND TREES ACROSS HIGHWAY 20/47 NEAR ASHTON/MARYSVILLE. FARM FIELDS FLOODED. SPUD CELLAR NORTH OF ASHTON HAS 3 FEET OF WATER IN IT.
TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR. VIDEO VIA KPVI SHOWS DAMAGE TO GARAGE AND CAMPER IN THE GROVELAND AREA ON THE WEST SIDE OF BLACKFOOT.
STILL FALLING AT TIME OF OBSERVATION.
SOME HAIL SLIGHTLY LARGER THAN GOLF BALL.
EIGHT LARGE WOODEN POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED OFF NEAR THE TOP, DROPPING POWER LINES AND TRANSFORMERS.
BIG TRRE BLOWN DOWN
URBAN STREET FLOODING BY COSTCO IN POCATELLO
FREMONT COUNTY EMERGENCY COORDINATOR REPORTED SMALLER STREAMS QUICKLY EXCEEDING THEIR BANKS. HE ALSO REPORTED SMALL HAIL COVERING THE GROUND IN MANY AREAS.
SEVERAL SMALL BRANCHES WERE BLOWN OUT OF TREES. NO SIZE GIVEN.
KETTLE BUTTE ARL SITE.
VERY HEAVY RAIN CONTINUING WITH SMALL HAIL COVERING THE GROUND. WATER RUNNING FROM PROPERTY ON TO ADJACENT ROAD.
61 MILE AN HOUR WIND GUST AT INL CENTRL FACILITY.
LOST RIVER REST AREA.
INL REACTOR TECH SITE.
CENTRAL INL FACILITY WIND SENSOR.
CENTRAL INL FACILITY WEATHER INSTRUMENT MEASURED GUST TO 67 MILES AN HOUR.
70 MILE AN HOUR WIND GUST AT CRITICAL INFRASTUCTURE COMPLEX INL.
BIG SOUTHERN BUTTE REGISTERED A WIND GUST TO 66 MILES AN HOUR.
ROAD DAMAGE AND CANELS READY TO OVERFLOW
BASEMENT IN NEW COURT HOUSE AT ARCO WAS FLOODED DO TO RAIN FROM THUNDERSTORM
0.50 INCH OF RAIN FELL IN 10 MINUTES.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS POPLAR TREES BLOWN DOWN
BLOWING DUST/LOW VISIBILITY HAS CLOSED I-15 FROM IDAHO FALLS TO ROBERTS AS OF NOON.
I-15 BETWEEN IDAHO FALLS AND ROBERTS IS CLOSED DUE TO BLOWING DUST
60 MPH SUSTAINED -- 75 MPH GUST
